channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania High Court Convicts Man in Husband's Murder Case
item.description: A Tanzanian man, Mwita Nyagabure, was convicted of murdering his neighbor John Habi after his wife, Pili Paul, testified that she witnessed the stabbing. The incident occurred on July 20, 2024, in Nya...

item.title: President Marcos commits to BARMM elections and peace initiatives
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has reaffirmed the government's commitment to advancing peace and development in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), emphasizing the importance ...

item.title: Reforming Migration Policies: South Africa's Approach to Border Security and Visa Regimes
item.description: The article argues that South Africa's focus on securitizing borders to curb irregular migration is ineffective and costly. It highlights that most undocumented migrants are actually documented indivi...

item.title: Manila Ticket Prices for Byeon Woo-seok's 'The Secret Library' Announced
item.description: ABS-CBN News reported the official release of ticket prices for Byeon Woo-seok's literary event 'The Secret Library,' scheduled to take place in Manila. The announcement, made on July 22, 2026, highli...

item.title: Petrol Pump Owners Postpone Strike After Government Negotiations
item.description: The All Pakistan Petroleum Pump Owners Association (APPPOA) has postponed its planned nationwide strike for two weeks following successful negotiations with Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik. Initi...

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio Avoids Question on Trump's Chinese Election Interference Claims
item.description: During a press conference in the Philippines, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio faced questions about whether he had discussed President Donald Trump's allegations of Chinese election interference w...

item.title: Ondo State Police Investigate Abduction of Two Farmers
item.description: Suspected gunmen have kidnapped two farmers in Ondo State's Akoko South-East Local Government Area, demanding a ransom of N10 million for their release. A family member revealed the incident during a ...

item.title: Canadians' Attitudes Toward AI Use in Education and Work Revealed in New Poll
item.description: A recent poll highlights Canadians' mixed attitudes toward artificial intelligence (AI) use in professional and academic settings. The survey reveals that most Canadians view AI integration in work an...

item.title: Kenyan TSC Teacher's Clean Payslip Sparks Debate on Financial Discipline
item.description: A photo of a Kenyan teacher's payslip showing zero loan deductions has gone viral, sparking debates about financial habits in the education sector. The image, shared on social media, highlights how un...

item.title: German Foreign Minister Advises Nigerian Youth on Skills and Language for Germany Opportunities
item.description: Germany’s Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul encouraged Nigerian youths to prepare for opportunities in the country’s labour market by developing relevant skills and learning the German language. He hig...

item.title: AFA Considers Legal Action Against Misinformation Campaign During 2026 World Cup
item.description: The Argentine Football Association (AFA) is considering legal action against individuals and organizations accused of spreading false allegations and manipulated images during the 2026 FIFA World Cup....

item.title: Young Footballer Joey Emmanuel Chad Dies at 20, Football Community Expresses Shock
item.description: Joey Emmanuel Chad, a 20-year-old rising football talent, has passed away, leaving the football community in shock. The young player had recently finalized a transfer to Swedish club Ytterhogdals IK f...

item.title: States Continue Using Current Emergency Systems Amid Aus Alert Rollout
item.description: Despite the federal government's $132 million investment in the Aus Alert system, Australian states will maintain their existing emergency warning systems for the upcoming bushfire season. The decisio...

item.title: India’s youth-led 'Cockroach' protests challenge Modi government over education reforms and exam leaks
item.description: India’s youth-led 'Cockroach' protests have escalated into a significant challenge for Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government, demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and...

item.title: Tanzanian Lawyers Highlight Legal Challenges in Prison Consultations for Tundu Lissu
item.description: Lawyers representing Tundu Lissu, the Chadema national chairman, have testified in court about restricted access to legal consultations while he is imprisoned at Ukonga Prison. The witnesses, Nashon N...

item.title: PML-N proposes constitutional change to appoint Nawaz Sharif as AJK chief minister
item.description: The Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari has alleged that the Pakistan Muslim League-Nawaz (PML-N) is pushing for a constitutional amendment to allow three-time former Prime Mi...

item.title: Chery Q Electric Hatchback Set to Launch in South Africa at R350k
item.description: Chery SA has confirmed the arrival of its new Q electric hatchback in South African showrooms by early September 2026. Positioned as the brand's most affordable new energy vehicle in the country, the ...

item.title: Israel's Land Sales in the West Bank to Jewish Americans Spark Controversy
item.description: The article details how Israeli real estate companies are facilitating the sale of land in the West Bank to Jewish American citizens, a practice critics argue exacerbates the Israeli-Palestinian confl...

item.title: Personal reflections on media nostalgia
item.description: The article compiles a series of nostalgic anecdotes from readers about their childhood experiences with television programs and technological changes. Paul Taylor jokes about the BYD car badge appear...

item.title: South Africa's Inflation Surpasses 5% in June, Prompting Possible Rate Hike
item.description: Consumer inflation in South Africa accelerated to 5% year-on-year in June, up from 4.5% in May, according to data released on Wednesday. This surge, driven primarily by transport costs (12.7%) and hou...
